chief_complaint: A 32-year-old pregnant woman presented with a new-onset seizure.
history: The patient had a history of an untreated dental abscess, which was identified as the source of a Streptococcus pneumoniae infection. She had no history of seizures, hypertension, or other medical conditions. She was not taking any medications. Her social history revealed poor dental care.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination revealed a tender dental abscess in the lower left molar region.
labs: CT scan of the head showed a brain abscess in the left frontal lobe. Blood cultures grew Streptococcus pneumoniae. ECG was normal.
correct_diagnosis: Brain abscess caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae from a dental source
differentials: Brain abscess caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae from a dental source
correct_ranking: Brain abscess caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae from a dental source
